risk_rationale: The court ruling reinforces the eSafety Commissioner's authority to issue notices, which may require TikTok to adhere to specific online safety expectations in Australia. This presents a moderate risk as it could necessitate adjustments to existing minor protection policies to align with these expectations.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potential requirement to adhere to specific online safety expectations for minors
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The ruling may introduce new expectations that affect age-gated account controls, requiring alignment with the eSafety Commissioner's notices.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Account privacy for 13-15 years: Private by default
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Potential requirement to adhere to specific online safety expectations for minors
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The ruling may necessitate changes to account privacy settings to meet new safety expectations.

remediations.title: Align with Basic Online Safety Expectations
remediations.description: Review and update minor protection policies to ensure compliance with the eSafety Commissioner's Basic Online Safety Expectations notices.
acceptance_criteria: Updated policies reflect compliance with eSafety Commissioner's notices
Account privacy settings for minors are reviewed and adjusted as necessary

business_impact: Potential need for policy adjustments could affect user onboarding and privacy settings for minors in Australia, impacting user experience and compliance costs.
recommended_actions: Engage with the eSafety Commissioner to understand specific expectations
Conduct a policy gap analysis to identify necessary changes
Implement policy updates and communicate changes to affected users
